name: lacI
annotation: DNA-binding transcriptional repressor LacI gene; encodes lactose operon repressor.
notes: Use as DNA Agent controlling LacI protein production.

name: lacZ
annotation: Gene encoding beta-galactosidase, part of lacZYA operon.
notes: Core reporter for lac operon induction.

name: lacY
annotation: Gene encoding lactose permease, part of lacZYA operon.
notes: Connects extracellular lactose to intracellular lactose availability.

name: lacA
annotation: Gene encoding galactoside O-acetyltransferase, part of lacZYA operon.
notes: Included for operon completeness.

name: crp
annotation: Gene encoding CRP/CAP, a cAMP receptor transcriptional regulator.
notes: CRP becomes an activator of lac expression when bound to cAMP.

name: cyaA
annotation: Gene encoding adenylate cyclase, which produces cAMP.
notes: Connects glucose state to cAMP availability in the coarse model.

name: lacO
annotation: lac operator regulatory DNA site bound by LacI in the repressed condition.
notes: Kept as a graph node so LacI-DNA binding can be represented explicitly.

name: LacI
annotation: LacI repressor binds the lac operator and represses lacZYA transcription; inducer binding reduces repression.
notes: State should respond to allolactose and lacO binding.

name: LacZ
annotation: Beta-galactosidase catalyzes beta-galactoside hydrolysis; associated with lactose utilization.
notes: In this coarse model, activity follows lacZ expression and lactose availability.

name: LacY
annotation: Lactose-proton symporter that imports lactose across the inner membrane.
notes: Important for coupling extracellular lactose to intracellular lactose.

name: LacA
annotation: Galactoside O-acetyltransferase encoded by lacA.
notes: Included for lacZYA operon completeness.

name: CRP
annotation: cAMP receptor protein; when bound to cAMP, participates in transcriptional activation of catabolite-sensitive genes including lac operon.
notes: Use complex.cAMP_CRP for activated regulatory form.

name: CyaA
annotation: Adenylate cyclase that produces cAMP from ATP.
notes: Coarse connection from glucose condition to cAMP level.

name: RNA polymerase
annotation: RNA polymerase transcribes expressed genes; included as a generic transcription machinery Agent for the lac prototype.
notes: Can later be split into subunits or sigma-specific forms.

name: Lactose
annotation: Disaccharide carbon source and inducer precursor for lac operon behavior.
notes: Extracellular lactose can become intracellular via LacY in the coarse model.

name: Allolactose
annotation: Inducer associated with lactose metabolism; binds LacI and reduces operator repression.
notes: KEGG ID not filled yet; avoid using wrong compound ID.

name: D-Glucose
annotation: Preferred carbon source; high glucose is associated with carbon catabolite repression in the coarse lac model.
notes: Used as an environmental nutrient state rather than a quantitative concentration.

name: cAMP
annotation: Second messenger that binds CRP/CAP to form an activating transcriptional regulator.
notes: In the coarse model, low glucose tends to increase cAMP availability.

name: cAMP-CRP
annotation: Activated CRP regulatory complex formed when cAMP binds CRP.
assembly_condition: CRP and cAMP are both available; cAMP binding forms the activated regulatory complex.
notes: Represents the active transcriptional activator form for lac operon.

name: LacI-lacO
annotation: Repressive complex formed when active LacI binds lac operator.
assembly_condition: Active LacI binds the lac operator when inducer effect is absent or low.
notes: When assembled, lacZYA should tend toward repressed state.
